decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana using Eldrazi Temple and Utopia Sprawl, while using control cards like Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to play powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and World Breaker as early as possible to dominate the board. It also includes cards like Sire of Seven Deaths and Ugin, Eye of the Storms for additional threats and removal spells. The deck's versatility and ability to quickly ramp up mana makes it a formidable control deck in Modern 1 vs 1 format.

This Jeskai Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature strategies combined with various support cards to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck aims to play efficient cheap creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, while also utilizing powerful removal like Solitude and Galvanic Discharge to control the board. Quantum Riddler provides card advantage and tempo, while Guide of Souls supports creature recursion. Teferi, Time Raveler disrupts opponent's game plan, while Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides a powerful finisher. The mana base is optimized for consistent casting of spells and creatures.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Allosaurus Rider combo by searching for and playing key pieces such as Summoner's Pact and Eldritch Evolution. By sacrificing Allosaurus Rider, players can search for and put powerful creatures like Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant, or Griselbrand onto the battlefield. The deck also includes cards like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer to protect the combo from disruption. Additionally, cards like Generous Ent, Atraxa, Grand Unifier, and Boseiju, Who Endures provide support and utility to help the player achieve their goal of winning through a powerful combo.

The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm your opponent with aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, supported by efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart. The deck also includes card draw and selection tools like Preordain and Mishra's Bauble to maintain momentum. The land base is optimized for consistency and speed, with fetch lands like Scalding Tarn and Wooded Foothills. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early and close out the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

This combo deck aims to use Amulet of Vigor to untap lands and generate infinite mana with the Urza's Saga land, allowing for quick ramping into Primeval Titan. Once Primeval Titan enters the battlefield, it can fetch lands like the powerful Urza's Saga or bounce lands like Simic Growth Chamber to keep the combo going. The end goal is to overwhelm the opponent with large creatures and powerful spells, using cards like Scapeshift and Malevolent Rumble to finish the game in a single explosive turn.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly swarm the opponent with low-cost aggressive creatures and burn spells, constantly applying pressure to whittle down their life total. It aims to close out the game as fast as possible by utilizing direct damage spells and efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear, Goblin Guide, and Eidolon of the Great Revel. The inclusion of cards like Lightning Bolt, Lava Spike, and Rift Bolt allows for additional reach and removal, while Chandra's Incinerator and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer provide powerful threats that can quickly end the game.

The deck revolves around quickly generating mana through rituals and medallions, while also gaining card advantage through spells like Reckless Impulse and Past in Flames. It aims to cast powerful sorceries like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Artist's Talent to create a storm of spells that overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of Wish allows for flexibility in accessing key cards from the sideboard. Boseiju, Who Endures ensures high impact spells can't be countered, while Valakut Awakening provides additional card draw and manipulation. Overall, the deck aims to execute a fast and explosive combo to win the game.

The main focus of this deck is to establish a powerful mana base with Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Mine, and Urza's Tower to quickly ramp into big threats such as Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. The deck utilizes control elements such as Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also using cards like Thought-Knot Seer and All Is Dust to remove key threats from the opponent's side of the field. Additionally, the deck features a mix of Eldrazi creatures for additional pressure and versatility.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through the efficient use of UrzaTron lands, which allows for the quick and consistent ramping into powerful late-game threats like Ugin, the Eye of Storms and Emrakul, the Promised End. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Thought-Knot Seer and Karn, the Great Creator along with removal spells such as Dismember and Kozilek's Command to help control the board. With the ability to quickly ramp into big threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan, this deck aims to dominate the late game and secure victory.

The deck focuses on aggressively ramping up artifact creatures and utilizing various artifacts and effects to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It includes a mix of cards that support the aggressive strategy, such as Arcbound Ravager and Galvanic Blast, as well as cards that disrupt the opponent's game plan, such as Engineered Explosives and Pithing Needle. The deck aims to quickly build a board presence using artifacts and efficient creatures, while also having the ability to control the game with counterspells and disruption.

The main focus of this Ruby Storm combo deck is to generate a large amount of red mana quickly by utilizing cards like Ruby Medallion and Pyretic Ritual. This mana is then used to cast powerful spells like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Grapeshot to deal massive damage to the opponent. Past in Flames allows for spells to be recast from the graveyard, ensuring a continuous stream of spells to overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Wrenn's Resolve and Hex Magic to protect the combo and maintain control of the game.

The deck focuses on cycling through cards to fill up the graveyard with creatures, then casting a cascade spell like Violent Outburst to bring them all back onto the battlefield from the graveyard at once. The key concept is to overwhelm the opponent with a sudden surge of powerful creatures, leveraging the interaction between cascade spells and creatures with cycling abilities. By efficiently managing resources and timing the cascade spells well, the deck aims to surprise and quickly defeat the opponent with a massive board presence.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on fast, aggressive plays to overwhelm the opponent quickly. Cards like Guide of Souls and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer provide early pressure while Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm disrupt the opponent's strategy. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer offer additional threats and card advantage. The inclusion of Goblin Bombardment allows for extra reach and potential damage. Overall, the deck aims to consistently apply pressure and close out games before the opponent can establish their own game plan.

This deck focuses on assembling a variety of creature options through tutoring effects and graveyard recursion, allowing for adaptability and flexibility in different matchups. By utilizing creatures like Young Wolf and Badgermole Cub alongside powerful cards like Yawgmoth, Thran Physician and Chord of Calling, the deck aims to generate value and control the board while setting up for a game-winning combo. The toolbox approach, combined with the combo potential of cards like Walking Ballista and Malevolent Rumble, gives the deck a versatile and resilient strategy against a variety of opponents.

This deck focuses on combining aggressive creature attacks with the ability to temporarily remove threats through flicker effects. The deck includes a mix of aggressive creatures like Witch Enchanter and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, along with disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The use of flicker effects like Flickerwisp and Ephemerate allow for flexibility in removing opposing threats or protecting your own creatures. Teferi, Time Raveler provides additional disruption, while cards like Solitude and Prismatic Ending offer versatile removal options. The deck aims to maintain board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures, then reanimating them for explosive plays. Cards like Faithless Looting and Thoughtseize help fuel the graveyard, while Unearth and Persist allow for bringing back creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones. The combo potential is further enhanced by cards like Psychic Frog and Abhorrent Oculus, creating a powerful engine that can overwhelm opponents. The deck aims to win through a combination of tempo plays and sudden bursts of power from its reanimated creatures.
